payload.text:
Act as an autonomous AI software engineer, mobile app developer, UI/UX designer, backend architect, and forensic science expert.

Your task is to FULLY DESIGN, BUILD, AND PREPARE FOR DEPLOYMENT a complete mobile application named "FORENOVA".

This is a ZERO-CONFIG project. Do not ask questions. Do not wait for user input.
Assume everything is approved. Generate everything automatically.

=================================================
APP OVERVIEW
=================================================
App Name: FORENOVA
Type: Forensic Science Learning + Evidence Handling Simulation App
Target Users: Forensic students, exam aspirants, beginners

=================================================
LOGIN & WELCOME
=================================================
• Simple login (username required)
• On login show animated message:
  "Welcome <User Name> to FORENOVA"
• Save user session locally

=================================================
UI / UX
=================================================
• Professional forensic theme
• Clean modern interface
• Dark Mode + Light Mode
• Bottom navigation tabs:
  Home | Evidence | Lab | Sketch | Quiz | Exams | Profile
• Smooth transitions & icons

=================================================
CORE MODULES (AUTO-IMPLEMENT ALL)
=================================================

1️⃣ HOME
• Welcome banner with username
• Quick access cards
• Learning progress bar

2️⃣ CRIME SCENE SKETCH & 3D MODEL
• Full crime-scene final sketch tool
• Drawing tools, scale, labels
• 3D crime scene builder
• Drag-and-drop elements
• Undo / Redo
• Export sketch & 3D model
• Save projects locally

3️⃣ EVIDENCE COLLECTION TRAINING
• Interactive simulation
• Tool selection per evidence type
• Step-by-step collection process
• Correct packaging method
• Photos & demo videos
• Error feedback if wrong method chosen

4️⃣ ASK AN EXPERT
• Chat interface
• Accept only forensic-related questions
• Automated expert responses
• Block unrelated queries

5️⃣ LAB ANALYSIS INFORMATION
• Evidence → Lab mapping
• Divisions:
  Biology, Ballistics, Toxicology, Cyber, Fingerprints
• Static nearby FSL / CFSL list
• Simple explanation of lab techniques

6️⃣ EVIDENCE PRESERVATION
• Storage rules for all evidence types
• Temperature guidelines
• Packaging material
• Safety precautions

7️⃣ KNOWLEDGE SECTION
• Famous forensic case studies
• Evidence database
• Quiz engine:
  MCQ, True/False, Image-based
• Score & feedback system

8️⃣ EXAM SECTION
• Previous year papers (PDF + MCQ)
• Static upcoming exam notifications
• Exam syllabus overview

9️⃣ PROFILE
• Username
• Progress summary
• Quiz scores
• Dark/Light mode toggle
• App info

=================================================
TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
=================================================
• Complete frontend UI screens
• Backend logic & data models
• Local database for user data
• Modular clean architecture
• Error handling
• Offline-friendly content
• Production-ready build

=================================================
FINAL OUTPUT (MANDATORY)
=================================================
Generate:
• Full app source code
• UI screen designs
• Navigation flow
• Backend logic
• Database structure
• Ready-to-deploy project

Do NOT explain.
Do NOT ask questions.
Do NOT skip features.

Just BUILD FORENOVA completely and output the finished application.

**Building FORENOVA App**

Okay, I'm deep into architecting "FORENOVA." The plan is solidifying. I'm focusing on a clean React/TypeScript setup with Tailwind CSS for efficiency, striving to meet the zero-config requirement.  Initial component structures are sketched out. Next steps involve outlining the key forensic modules.


**Implementing AI Integration**

Now, I'm focusing on the "Ask an Expert" feature. The Gemini API implementation strategy is taking shape. It'll be a forensics-focused chatbot. I've also finalized the core modules like Sketch/3D, Evidence Collection, and Lab Analysis. I'm exploring an interactive step-by-step training for evidence collection.  Additionally, I have outlined features such as Login/Welcome and Navigation. The UI/UX design is coming together, too, with dark/light mode and a forensic theme. The knowledge and exam sections are also scoped.


**Planning the Sketch Tool**

The 2D canvas API is the selected method for sketching, for robustness. This approach will simulate a 3D scene builder by layering 2D top-down views and assets to approximate a 3D environment.  The Expert feature will use Gemini 3 Flash Preview for focused forensic question-answering. The groundwork is there, now it's time to build.

diagnostics.message: Argument of type '"quiz"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'TabType'.

payload.text:
**Identifying the Error**

Okay, I've zoned in on the error: `modules/Dashboard.tsx` line 68. The issue is a type mismatch. "quiz" isn't a valid `TabType` according to `types.ts`. I need to fix this.


**Correcting the Mismatch**

I've got it! The `TabType` enum in `types.ts` is the culprit. `'quiz'` isn't included, and it should actually map to the `<QuizEngine />` component. So, I'm fixing the `onClick` handler in `Dashboard.tsx` to set the active tab to `'library'` instead of `'quiz'`. This should align with how it's used in `App.tsx` and fix the type mismatch I found earlier.


**Examining Gemini Service**

I'm now diving into `services/geminiService.ts`. I've verified the import, initialization, model usage, content extraction, and call are all correct according to the project's guidelines. I did, however, notice that the `GoogleGenAI` instance is created *inside* the `getExpertResponse` function. I need to make sure this meets the project's requirement to create a fresh instance before each API call, as the instructions state this is to ensure the latest API key is used.


**Refining API Usage**

I'm now revisiting the API key usage in `services/geminiService.ts`. I noted the code currently uses `process.env.API_KEY || ""`. While functional, the instructions highlight the need to access the API key directly from the environment variable. I'm examining this, aiming for stricter adherence to the project guidelines. I'm also double-checking how the user prompt is constructed and passed to ensure that the content is being passed as `contents`.


**Pinpointing the Solution**

Okay, I've confirmed that the issue is still a type mismatch on line 68 of `Dashboard.tsx`.  The problem is `setActiveTab('quiz')`, which isn't a valid `TabType`.  I'll correct this by changing it to `setActiveTab('library')`. This will align with the component mapping in `App.tsx`. I've also verified the API key usage in `geminiService.ts` and will adjust it to access the environment variable directly. I've re-examined the Gemini API service, including its imports, model, content handling and the API call. No changes were necessary.


**Optimizing Gemini Configuration**

I'm now revising the configuration in `services/geminiService.ts` to fully align with the `@google/genai` guidelines. I've switched from using the fallback `process.env.API_KEY || ""` to accessing the API key directly via `process.env.API_KEY`, a minor but significant improvement. I've also re-examined the existing configuration, removing extraneous `systemInstruction` and clarifying `config`.


**Validating the Fix**

I'm now going over the change to confirm it aligns with the project's setup. The code now uses the correct type, `library`, for tab selection. I'm satisfied that this directly addresses the error and maintains the project's logic and structure. I'm preparing to test this and verify the results.

I've been making sure these UI components accurately and consistently reflect user session data. I'm verifying that the progress percentage and bar visually align, and also that the correct trophy image is consistently displayed. I've re-evaluated that the user interface updates are dynamic in response to data changes.


**Reviewing Notifications Display**

I'm now deeply engaged in the `NotificationItem` component.  I'm meticulously reviewing its structure, making sure the `title`, `time`, and `tag` are displayed correctly. I'm focusing on their presentation, ensuring they are both clear and visually appealing. I'm also confirming the notifications are styled consistently with the project's overall design language and verifying that the `tag` component receives the expected `tag` prop from the parent.


**Verifying Tab Functionality**

I'm now focusing on the `Knowledge Base Teaser` component within the `UserDashboard`. I'm verifying the button click, which calls `setActiveTab('lab')`, functions correctly and switches the view as expected. I'm confirming that the `lab` tab correctly displays and integrates within the tab system. I'm also ensuring the component looks correct with the added changes.


**Revising UI Styles**

I'm now inspecting `NotificationItem` and `KnowledgeBaseTeaser` components. I'm focusing on UI consistency, ensuring font sizes, colors, and margins align with the broader project design. I'm also ensuring the notification component follows project design language. I've reevaluated the styling of the `tag` component and the `KnowledgeBaseTeaser` button styles. I'm working to unify the UI of the tab system to maintain consistency throughout the dashboard.


**Enhancing API Integration**

I'm presently refactoring the Gemini service initialization to use `process.env.API_KEY` directly, ensuring alignment with the latest SDK requirements.  This improves the setup to provide better security, and to ensure smooth operation according to the project’s specifications. I'm focusing on adapting the integration for optimum API key usage, verifying that it aligns with the updated best practices.

**Refining API Setup**

I'm now revising the integration with the Google Gemini API. I have improved the code to now retrieve the API key directly using `process.env.API_KEY`, which is the correct approach. I've also validated that the system instruction is being correctly integrated with the existing configuration parameters.

**Realigning the Key**

I'm now integrating the API key usage directly from `process.env.API_KEY` for enhanced security and compliance. This change also fixes the prior code which had fallen behind best practices. I'm now confirming the active tab in `Dashboard.tsx` uses the 'library' tab, which aligns with the desired behavior. The `<QuizEngine />` component will now be rendered when the 'library' tab is selected. This ensures accurate tab handling and component rendering. **Considering Expansion Options**

I'm currently focused on expanding the "Case Library" within FORENOVA. I've pinpointed two key areas for enhancement: adding four new book options and improving navigation from the reading view. My initial assessment centers on the `constants.dart` file where I'll begin adding the book entries. I plan to use the current structure to create consistent and easy to access book reading.


**Expanding Case Library Further**

I'm now integrating four additional cases into the system: Night Stalker, Lindbergh Kidnapping, Harold Shipman, and Green River Killer. I'm focusing on updating `constants.tsx` to include these new entries and their relevant forensic details. Additionally, I'm working on the `Library.tsx` file to implement a prominent back button at the top of the reading view to enhance navigation, as it's something the user requested.


**Enhancing Case Navigation**

I'm currently finalizing the navigation refinements in `Library.tsx`. I've added the back button to the top of the reading view, which addresses the user feedback. The case data is ready; I'll incorporate the new cases: The Night Stalker, Lindbergh Kidnapping, Harold Shipman, and Green River Killer.

**Developing Navigation Flow**

I'm currently updating the `types.ts` file to include `sourceUrl` and `relatedCaseIds` for each case study.  Next, I'll revise `constants.tsx` to pre-populate these fields. This integration should complete the user's information flow.
